section.data-narration: A device special file is a type of file that serves as an interface between the operating system and hardware devices.
section.text: A device special file is a file on disk that has special permissions and attributes that allow it to be used as a device.
It is a symbolic link to a device driver or a block device that provides access to a hardware device.

section.data-narration: There are two types of device special files: block device special files and character device special files.
section.text: Block device special files represent block devices such as hard drives, tape drives, and solid-state drives.
Character device special files represent character devices such as printers, terminals, and network interfaces.

section.data-narration: Examples of device special files include /dev/sda, /dev/tty0, /dev/null, and /dev/zero.
section.text: Examples of device special files include /dev/sda, /dev/tty0, /dev/null, and /dev/zero.

section.data-narration: Device special files are created by the operating system when a hardware device is detected.
section.text: Device special files are created by the operating system when a hardware device is detected.
